topics.title: Europe braces for record heat
topics.summary: A severe early-summer heatwave is pushing parts of Europe past 40 C, disrupting travel, schools and outdoor events while officials urge people to protect vulnerable neighbours.
topics.discussionPrompt: Heatwaves really change daily routines. What is your best low-tech trick for staying cool when the weather gets unreasonable?

topics.title: Canada advances despite Swiss loss
topics.summary: Switzerland beat Canada 2-1 in Vancouver to top their World Cup group, but Canada is still headed to its first knockout-stage match, now set for Inglewood, California.
topics.discussionPrompt: Canada moving on despite a loss feels very sports-like. Is it better to enter the next round humbled, or riding a winning streak?

topics.title: Chip news lifts global markets
topics.summary: Global tech stocks rose after strong updates from Micron and Qualcomm revived enthusiasm around AI-related demand, while falling oil prices helped ease some inflation worries.
topics.discussionPrompt: AI keeps showing up in market stories. Do you think investors are seeing real change, or just getting swept up in another big tech wave?

topics.title: Bank of Canada keeps options open
topics.summary: Bank of Canada minutes show policymakers want to stay flexible as they weigh weak growth, trade uncertainty and recent energy-driven inflation pressure after holding rates at 2.25%.
topics.discussionPrompt: Central banks always sound cautious. Do you think steady rates make people feel more confident, or just more unsure about what comes next?
